_about: Qwen2.5-7B KV-quant rescue/diagnosis sweep + NF4-vs-uniform mechanism probe. Same harness (tq_paper_lb_shard.py). All on Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, LongBench trec/triviaqa/qasper, full 200 (uniform4 qasper pending at time of writing).

nf4_k4v8.verdict: COLLAPSE (values not the cause; keys are)

uniform_3bit.verdict: HEALTHY (fewer bits than NF4, still works)

uniform_4bit.verdict: HEALTHY (DECISIVE: same bits as failing NF4, only codebook differs)

mechanism.nf4_recon_error_similar_across_models: Measured NF4 vs uniform4 per-channel relerr on real pre-RoPE keys (L0/L10/L20). NF4 relerr ~0.08 at L10/L20 for BOTH Qwen and Llama -> NF4 does NOT represent Qwen keys worse. The split is error TOLERANCE, not representation.
mechanism.nf4_4x_worse_than_uniform: NF4 (zero-centered, symmetric absmax) costs ~3-5x more error than asymmetric uniform (min/max) at equal 4 bits, on BOTH models, because KV keys have a DC offset (per-group range/absmax ~0.9) and NF4 wastes ~half its codes on the empty side.
mechanism.qwen_error_sensitive: Qwen2.5-7B has 7:1 GQA (28 query : 4 KV heads); each key error feeds 7 query heads. Llama-2-7B is 1:1 MHA. Qwen collapses above a key-relerr threshold between ~0.04 (uniform3) and ~0.08 (nf4-4bit); Llama's threshold is >0.08.
mechanism.model_dependence: No universal best codebook. Llama: NF4 > uniform (20.82 vs ~15.3 qasper). Qwen: uniform >> NF4 (collapse). Mistral (4:1 GQA): NF4 fine.
